trade.summary: India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, textiles, chemicals, and IT services to Mexico. Mexico exports minerals, vehicles, machinery, and agricultural products. India has a consistent trade surplus. Both countries are manufacturing hubs with complementary supply chain roles.

diplomatic.summary: India and Mexico maintain normal diplomatic relations and interact primarily at G20 and UN forums. Both are large democracies and emerging economies. Bilateral engagement is thin relative to the size of both economies. India has a strong diaspora in the US but limited presence in Mexico. Diplomatic bandwidth is constrained by geographic distance.

military.summary: No significant military relationship. Occasional exchanges at multilateral defence forums.

financial.summary: Indian IT and pharmaceutical companies have expanded into Mexico as a nearshore market. Infosys, TCS, and Wipro serve US clients from Mexico. Some Indian manufacturing companies have explored Mexican presence for US market access.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. India has explored Mexican oil imports but geography makes it uncompetitive. Some cooperation on renewable energy technology.

scientific.summary: Limited but growing scientific cooperation. Joint research programmes in biotechnology and IT. Indian student presence in Mexico is growing.
